program.name: Head Start
program.description:
Head Start is a free program for children ages 3 to 5. It offers preschool classes, meals, health checkups, and support for child development and families. Families pay nothing to join.

Families with low income can apply. Children who get SNAP (food stamps), TANF (cash help), or SSI (disability benefits) qualify right away. Children in foster care or experiencing homelessness also qualify. Children with disabilities are given priority for open spots.

If your child is 5 years old, they must not yet be old enough to start kindergarten. Each school district sets its own cutoff date for kindergarten. If you are not sure, contact your local Head Start program to find out if your child still qualifies.

Some local programs also have spots for families whose income is a little above the poverty limit. If your income is slightly over the limit, contact your local Head Start program to ask about open spots.

To apply, talk to your local Head Start program. They'll help you find out if your child qualifies, give you the forms you need, and tell you what documents you'll need to submit. If there is not a space for your child, you can ask to be added to the waiting list.
program.description_short: Free preschool for children ages 3 to 5
program.website_description: Free preschool, meals, and health services for children ages 3 to 5 from low-income families

documents.text: Proof of home address (ex: lease, utility bill)
Proof of identity for child (ex: birth certificate)
Proof of income (ex: pay stubs, tax returns, benefit award letters)
Child's immunization records (can be provided within 90 days of enrollment)
Proof of TANF, SSI, or SNAP benefits if applicable
